DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE (TRANSACTION ID # 57593820)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER DISTRICT COUNCIL OF IRON WORKERS' MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. A PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD MAY BE FILED WITHIN 4 YEARS AFTER THE SERVICE OF THE ARBITRATION AWARD. (CCP 1288.) HOWEVER, A MOTION TO VACATE OR CORRECT AN AWARD MUST BE FILED WITHIN 100 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF THE AWARD. (ID.) FURTHER, CCP 1288.2 PROVIDES THAT "[A] RESPONSE REQUESTING THAT AN AWARD BE VACATED OR THAT AN AWARD BE CORRECTED SHALL BE SERVED AND FILED NOT LATER THAN 100 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF SERVICE OF A SIGNED COPY OF THE AWARD UPON" THE RESPONDENT. THE 100 DAY TIME PERIOD IS JURISDICTIONAL, AND THE COURT HAS NO POWER TO EXTEND THAT MANDATORY DEADLINE. (ABERS V. ROHRS (2013) 217 CAL.APP.4TH 1199, 1211.) HERE, THE AWARD WAS SERVED ON RESPONDENT ON OR ABOUT MARCH 2, 2015. (SEE DECLARATION OF JAMES HUSEBO, PG. 3.) THE TIME TO HAVE THE AWARD CORRECTED OR VACATED THEREFORE EXPIRED ON JUNE 10, 2015, 100 DAYS AFTER MARCH 2, 2015. THUS, RESPONDENT'S OPPOSITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AND REQUEST THAT THE COURT VACATE THE AWARD, FILED ON JULY 13, 2015, IS UNTIMELY. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/SB
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-24-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER DISTRICT COUNCIL OF IRON WORKERS' MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD is granted. A petition to confirm arbitration award may be filed within 4 years after the service of the Arbitration Award. (CCP 1288.) However, a motion to vacate or correct an award must be filed within 100 days after service of the award. (Id.) Further, CCP 1288.2 provides that "[a] response requesting that an award be vacated or that an award be corrected shall be served and filed not later than 100 days after the date of service of a signed copy of the award upon" the respondent. The 100 day time period is jurisdictional, and the court has no power to extend that mandatory deadline. (Abers v. Rohrs (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1211.) Here, the award was served on Respondent on or about March 2, 2015. (See Declaration of James Husebo, Pg. 3.) The time to have the award corrected or vacated therefore expired on June 10, 2015, 100 days after March 2, 2015. Thus, Respondent's opposition to confirm arbitration and request that the court vacate the award, filed on July 13, 2015, is untimely. Request for attorneys' fees is denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb
